source-discipline.reason: DR is grounded in three specific failure modes named from the conversation prior to its authorship: (a) anchor sources whose original tier is correct but whose evidence has been superseded since publication, (b) Co-1 evidence treatment ambiguity where the mission says co-primary but supersession semantics differ from Tier 1, (c) the temporal dimension of doctrinal commitment 2 — best practice is determined by the operative evidence hierarchy, which has a recency component the prior protocol did not check. No invented failure modes; each cited to either mission text or prior-turn discussion.

canonical-workplan.reason: DR refines what 'citation_mining_complete' means without altering the workplan structure (per PI rule #6). The v2 closure bar adds a precondition to slug closure; it does not change the workplan §B.11 scope or phase ordering. Phase B work continues; no Phase E synthesis authored as part of this DR.

evidence-verification-gate-for-synthesis.reason: Supersession candidates are recorded by DOI in superseding_dois (not auto-INSERTed to evidence_sources) until verified per PI rule #10. The skill file §4 makes this explicit: 'rule #10 forbids INSERTing candidates as evidence_sources rows without verification.' The DR complements rule #10 with a temporal-and-tier dimension (current-best) but does not weaken the existence-and-content gate.

adherence-logging-and-attestation.reason: Skill file §7 explicitly requires [READ: DR-2026-05-24] tag + per-slug [STAGE:] boundaries; the skill cannot be invoked without contributing to the audit trail. Semiannual sweeps additionally require enumerating skipped records as [GAP:] tags.

best-practice-supersession.reason: This DR introduces the rule. It is in-scope for itself by definition — the DR is the authorizing instrument.

deviations.reason: Co-1 supersession treated as accumulation rather than replacement (co1_addition_logged outcome). Owner confirmation pending. Schema reversibly supports parallel-to-Tier-1 treatment if directed. Evidence: decisions/DR-2026-05-24-best-practice-supersession-protocol.md#co-1-supersession-treatment

bias_direction: I authored this DR after the owner named the failure mode (anchors going stale without recency check). My natural pull is toward protocol-design completeness — adding the semiannual sweep cadence + the closure-definition-version stamp + the 5-outcome enum + the search-strategy matrix is more infrastructure than the minimum the failure mode required. A more parsimonious DR would have just added supersession_check_complete=1 as a v2 closure requirement and deferred the semiannual cadence + schema versioning to a follow-up. The bias direction is over-engineering the first pass at protocol infrastructure; the counter-argument is that under-engineering would have left obvious gaps that a follow-up DR would just fill in. [RE-ATTESTATION 2026-06-11 per PI rule #11a: originally attested by session_2026-05-23-bpc-rewrite-phase-b-closure at doctrine 61c7f95; re-checked and re-attested at 3da73bd by session_2026-06-11-stage-4.3-gate-closure after doctrine delta 3fb2882 (Stage-1.1 tier-hierarchy revision: SR/meta T3->T2, Tier 1 expanded to admit directly-relevant high-control non-OT primary research, Tier 3 redefined as lower-control primary, Tiers 4-6 walled off). Assessment: Protocol substance (best practice must rest on current, non-superseded evidence) is hierarchy-agnostic and complies. Tier-LABEL references ('Tier 1-3', 'Tier-3 systematic reviews') reflect pre-Stage-1.1 numbering; under 3da73bd SR/meta is Tier 2, not Tier 3. Stale reference, not a compliance defect; point-in-time DR not retrofitted.] [RE-ATTESTATION 2026-07-13 per Mode 3 item 3 (skills/integrity-protocol_SKILL.md): the Q14 adversarial pass on the 2026-07-13 ratification execution (workplan/ratification-execution-register-2026-07-13.md, "Q14 — independent adversarial pass" section, B-scope finding 2/3) independently re-verified this DR's substance and found it sound: migration 015 applied, all 6 Pass-2 retroactive audits complete at closure_definition_version='v2' (cognitive-wayfinding-design, mental-health-built-environment, mobility-built-environment, room-acoustic-performance, school-environment-autism, stair-ramp-threshold-biomechanics-accessibility) -- confirming this attestation's own independent_reviewer_counterclaim point (b)'s empirical-validation bet paid off. The finding was process, not substance: the DR's status flip to ACCEPTED on 2026-07-13 had not been synced to its own footer Status section, which still read PROPOSED with unmet conditions -- corrected in the same commit as this re-attestation.]
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer would observe: (a) the Co-1 supersession decision is an [ASSUMPTION], not an owner-confirmed decision, so the DR is technically incomplete on a substantive doctrinal point until that's confirmed; (b) the search strategy matrix names connectors (PubMed, Scholar Gateway, Cochrane) without empirically validating that they each return useful supersession candidates on a real anchor source — the matrix is theoretical; (c) the closure_definition_version stamp adds complexity to the schema that wouldn't be needed if all slugs were just re-audited to v2 directly. Counter: (a) the [ASSUMPTION] is named explicitly and the schema reversibly supports both treatments; (b) Pass 2 (the 6 retroactive audits) is the empirical validation — if PubMed/Scholar Gateway return nothing useful, the protocol fails the validation test and gets revised; (c) closure_definition_version is the audit-trail mechanism that lets us answer 'when did this slug last meet the current best-practice bar' — without the stamp, we lose the version history of the closure semantic itself.

reattestation.reason: The weak-band delta now permits a code-only cell to render as flagged weak 'best practice'. This DR's supersession rule (prefer current best evidence over a superseded source) is orthogonal to that rendering change and still holds — a superseded source is still in error. Verdict unchanged.
